points.text: Common symptoms include coughing, fever, and loss of smell and taste.
Symptoms may also include headaches, nasal congestion, and muscle pain.
Breathing difficulties can occur in moderate to severe cases.
Fever, fatigue, cough, and breathing difficulties are often reported.
Loss of taste combined with loss of smell is reported in up to 88% of cases.
A respiratory symptom cluster includes cough, sputum, and shortness of breath.
Muscle and joint pain, headache, and fatigue are part of a musculoskeletal symptom cluster.
Digestive symptoms can include abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea.
COVID-19 symptoms often show up 2 to 14 days after contact with the virus.
Common symptoms include congestion, runny nose, or sore throat.
Other symptoms include dry cough and shortness of breath.
Loss of taste or smell is also a common symptom.
Extreme tiredness, known as fatigue, can occur.
Digestive symptoms like upset stomach, vomiting, or diarrhea are possible.
Pain, including headaches and body or muscle aches, may be present.
Fever or chills are also common symptoms of COVID-19.
Dizziness and dry mouth can be symptoms of COVID-19.
Confusion or agitation may indicate a serious symptom requiring help.
The most common symptoms are fever, cough, and tiredness.
Other symptoms can include shortness of breath, chest pain or pressure, and muscle or body aches.
Headache, loss of taste or smell, confusion, and sore throat are also common symptoms.
Congestion or runny nose, diarrhea, nausea and vomiting, and abdominal pain may occur.
Skin rashes can be a symptom of COVID-19.
Infants may experience difficulty feeding as a symptom.
